fields.description:
Surgeons would like a way to interact and review a 3D version of their CORIOGRAPH knee plans prior to surgery. Surgeons are interested in seeing cross-sectional views. 

Surgeons may interface directly with this 3D plan or a rep may “run the screen” while a surgeon overlooks. This may happen in the OR as the patient is being prepped, or may happen outside of the OR in the ~10 minutes before surgery starts. 

Surgeons and reps expect that any changes made to the plans (position, sizing) will be reflected in intraop, upon entering the planning state.
fields.summary: Knee Planner

fields.description:
Evaluate CORIOGRAPH software processes and identify and implement efficiency improvements where possible.



* Update Segaway(HiRes) to automatically move case folders from Image drop to PMP for all case types.
* Update Segaway to include functionality for image set verification of complete image set and comparison of DICOM header to the protocol.
* Update InfoVis functionality to display image verification results from Segaway.
* -Possible inclusion of knee specific updates for InfoVis if not included in SPOP scope.-
* Upgrade hardware for Segaway, Plexus, and Dendron hybrid servers. (MS4)
* Plexus and Dendron performance enhancements for creation and parsing of SR documents.
* Institute process efficiencies to include CI/CD support and increased implementation of  automated testing.
* Increase unit test coverage for Segway, Plexus, and Dendron.
* Tool and Libraries Update

fields.description:
A feature (AI model) that leverages previously completed CORI case data to inform initial femur/tibia implant positions and sizes in Combined Planning. The idea is that by looking at previous case data (implant type, landmark data, preop gap data, and femur/tibia implant placement plans and sizes), an algorithm can be developed to enable CORI to better predict where a surgeon may want to place the femur and tibia implants when compared to the CORI default algorithms or SPP. 

The expectation is that this feature will be a “second version” of SPP (as in, will be replacing SPP).
fields.summary: AI Planning Feature in TKA

fields.description:
We need the ability to assign multiple roles to a single user profile. RAR user profile is for insights data upload and surgeon support, while sales rep role in NSYNC is created to suuport pre-op plans, for users who play both roles for an account we should provide access for those users to all pages for both roles.

A user can play multiple roles in the Cumulus system. They could be both admin and customer service and RAR and Sale rep. Additional user roles are conditional to the first user role selected. If RAR is selected then only Sales rep is an additional option, the same works for Customer service and admin 

* If RAR is selected first, Sales Rep can be added.
* If Sales Rep is selected first, RAR can be added.
* If Admin is selected first, Customer service can be added.
* If Customer service is selected first, Admin can be added. 
* Admin and Customer Service
** Dashboard Changes: No changes
** Page Options: Show pre-op plans page
* RAR and Sales Rep.
** Dashboard Changes: Add a new tab on the dashboard to show pre-op plan widgets- Customer and pending case count.

Page Options: Show pre-op plans page on the left navigation screen.
fields.summary: Multi Role per user profile

fields.description:
{color:#ff5630}*User Facing text needs to align with final product naming and terminology.*{color}

*LOGGING NEEDS (Navio Logs)*
Add information to log file that denotes case as image-based 


*SURGEON FACING RI.INSIGHTS NEEDS* *(including casedata.json)*
Assumption: CORI team will create UKA casedata.json so that UKA data can be displayed in RI.INSIGHTS at the point in time when RI.INSIGHTS enables UKA case data display

1) ”Behind-the-scenes” checkbox/filter to sort image-based outcomes (filter on planning averages)
_Note: messaging should not indicate that IF is more or less accurate than IB and vice versa, implementation should not lead to comparisons between IF/IB outcomes_

2) Add tag for image-based case in TKA/UKA casedata.json (presence of data in field (data = Visionaire case ID will indicate image-based case)

3) Add tag for SPP in TKA casedata.json (helps us understand how impactful SPP is in terms of time spent in planning)

4) Change status of plan based on Visionaire Case ID


*CORI/NSYNC STORAGE NEEDS*
Assumption(s): 1) no storage concerns on CORI/NSYNC, 2) Visionaire expiration data: 6 months from date of imaging

After 6 months, do we 1) prevent users from using data outright or 2) do we need to notify users about expiration? Need to understand patient risk associated with these options. 5/10: leading option = #2
fields.summary: RI Insights ONLY - Pre-Op Planning - Post-Operative Use of Data

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
h2. Threat Scenario

The RI.Insights application APIs use a cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) policy that allows access from any domain. This may allow a third party threat actor to retrieve sensitive data from a session or perform privileged actions on behalf of the user.

h2. Potential Impact

An Internet-based threat actor could target users of the RI.Insightsapplication by embedding script payloads or abusing Cross-Site Scripting vulnerabilities on other, often trusted, websites. When the payload is run in the browser of the target user, it could direct requests to the Cumulus application; if the target user is already authenticated to RI.Insights, the threat actor would be able to covertly issue and retrieve the results of the malicious requests as if they were issued legitimately by the authenticated user.

h2. Threat Discovery

An anonymous Internet-based threat actor could discover this vulnerable condition by sending a request to RI.Insightswith a custom HTTP Origin header containing the domain of their choice. For instance, an attacker could create an HTTP Origin header containing 'Origin: [https://attack.l9group.com|https://attack.l9group.com/]' and then observe the response from the server that appends the chosen domain of the threat actor to the trusted Origin list.

h2. Affected Components

case-Release-2.1-master\src\main\java\org\snn\api\ApiOriginFilter.java
faq-service-Release-2.1-master\src\main\java\org\snn\api\ApiOriginFilter.java
file-transfer-service-Release-2.1-master\src\main\java\org\snn\api\ApiOriginFilter.java
hipdataprocess-Release-2.1-master\src\main\java\org\snn\api\ApiOriginFilter.java
logger-service-Release-2.1-master\src\main\java\org\snn\ApiOriginFilter.java
logger-service-Release-2.1-master\src\main\java\org\snn\api\ApiOriginFilter.java
notification-service-Release-2.1-master\src\main\java\org\snn\api\ApiOriginFilter.java
procedure-metadata-Release-2.1-master\src\main\java\org\snn\api\ApiOriginFilter.java
users-Release-2.1-master\src\main\java\org\snn\api\ApiOriginFilter.java

h2. Remediation Recommendation

Create a CORS policy that only allows a trusted list of domains.

h2. Technical Details

The assessment team initially discovered this issue after noting the wildcard Origin HTTP header explicitly set in the API filter source code. To validate the presence of the issue on the provided environment, comprehensive mapping of application assets and API endpoints was conducted. Each discovered asset or endpoint was then actively requested with a provided Origin header containing a wildcard or a domain specified and controlled by the assessment team. Each HTTP response was analyzed to determine how the server platform reacted to the Origin payload and in this case, affirming the source code discovery of the vulnerability, the application server responded with Access-Control-Allow-Origin header containing the wildcard value.
fields.summary: CORS Arbitrary Origin

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
h2. Threat Scenario

Multiple runtime dependencies of the application are outdated and one bundled library contains high-risk vulnerabilities. Vulnerabilities that reside deep in dependencies or Third-Party Components (TPCs) typically survive in an application attack surface for extended periods of time. In contrast to vulnerabilities in custom application code, vulnerabilities in TPCs, once discovered publicly, are exploited in large horizontal operations by threat actors. Note that in this case, the affected component is currently listed as End-Of-Life (EOL) from the software vendor and security updates may not be available for this version.

h2. Potential Impact

Containers that do not prevent applications from running as USER root could allow a threat actor who has gained access to the container to run code as a privileged (root) user. Applications running as root in the container that have been exploited could be used by the threat actor to execute commands / actions as the root user via the exploited application.

h2. Threat Discovery

The assessment team examined the affected Docker configuration files and noted that commands are run as the USER root. The USER attribute is not set to a lower privilege user after the commands are run, resulting in the container running with root privileges. A threat actor that has compromised the containerized application could discern the process privilege attained.

h2. Affected Components

s3-java-unzipper-eb-Release-2.1-master\Dockerfile

h2. Remediation Recommendation

After running the required privileged commands as the 'root' user, drop privileges by switching the user context to a normal unprivileged user for running the application.

h2. Technical Details

The assessment team examined the affected Docker configuration files and noted that commands are run as the USER root. The USER attribute is not set to a lower privilege user after the commands are run, resulting in the container running with root privileges.
fields.summary: Docker container runs as USER root
